data.text: strength of mind or spirit that enables a person to encounter danger with firmness: personal bravery

data.id: World War II Valor in the Pacific National Monument:g
data.text: park comprising nine sites in Hawaii, California, and Alaska

enrichment.etymology: Middle English valour "worth, worthiness, bravery," borrowed from Anglo-French valor, valur, inherited or borrowed from early Medieval Latin valor, from Latin val- (stem of validus "in good health, robust, having legal authority," valēre "to be well, have strength") + -or, noun suffix ,
